product_summary: Refactor the run-template from a sequential scaffold generator into a compiler-oriented runtime with structured IR, deterministic caching, verified execution, and content-addressed experiment workspaces.

constitution.title: Factory Compiler Refactor Constitution
constitution.what_it_is: This project is a compiler-style execution runtime for research-driven factory work. It must lower requests into structured IR, enforce explicit file authorization and verification contracts, and execute changes through idempotent, replayable stages rather than a sequential shell script.
core_loop: A task request is lowered into a validated frontend IR with explicit file authorization.
The pipeline enriches that request with constitution and verification contracts, then compiles an execution plan.
Execution either replays a verified cached patch or runs an isolated experiment, verifies the result, and records the full event history.
positioning: Prefer deterministic compiler primitives over prompt-only orchestration.
Do not silently mutate shared state or allow unauthorized file writes.
Treat verification evidence and compensation paths as first-class runtime outputs.

items.label: IR-first orchestration
items.text: Execution must be driven by typed intermediate representations, not ad hoc script arguments or implicit globals.

items.label: Authorized writes only
items.text: Every execution plan must restrict file mutations to an explicit repository-relative whitelist.

items.label: Idempotent execution
items.text: A repeated run with identical frontend inputs, base commit, and constitution must be able to replay from cache.

items.label: Verified outputs
items.text: No patch is admissible unless targeted fail-to-pass checks fail on the base state and pass-to-pass checks succeed on the patched state.

primitives.name: FrontendIR
primitives.summary: The validated task schema containing task identity, base commit, and authorized file scope.

primitives.name: MiddleEndIR
primitives.summary: The enriched task including constitution and explicit verification contracts.

primitives.name: ExecutionPlan
primitives.summary: The final lowering stage that binds model choice, sandbox type, and execution resource limits.

primitives.name: Verified Patch Artifact
primitives.summary: A replayable patch plus verification evidence and event history.
primitives.current_status: Implemented through cache, verifier, and event store runtime modules.

file_ownership.description: Compiler runtime, IR, cache, pipeline, verification, watcher, and worktree logic.
Scaffold generators and CLI entrypoints that bridge the runtime into the existing run-kit flow.
Unit tests covering deterministic cache behavior, authorization, saga compensation, and pipeline orchestration.

done_criteria.name: Compiler Runtime
criteria: A task can be lowered to all three IR levels without implicit fields.
The pipeline can replay a verified patch from cache or execute through isolated workspace stages.
Unauthorized writes, verification failures, and cleanup actions are visible in structured runtime outputs.

debt.title: Factory Compiler Runtime Slices
debt.intro: > These work items map the compiler-spec refactor into execution slices that can be emitted as tickets and verified locally.

work_items.title: Define typed compiler IR and lowering boundaries
ground_truth: The template previously lowered configuration into markdown and YAML artifacts only.
The runtime needs typed FrontendIR, MiddleEndIR, and ExecutionPlan stages.
scope: Create strict Pydantic models for frontend, middle-end, and backend lowering stages.
Model verification contracts, sandbox type, and resource limits.
Normalize authorized files into a deterministic whitelist.
acceptance: The IR forbids unknown fields.
Authorized file lists are normalized and deduplicated.
Execution plans serialize deterministically.

work_items.title: Implement deterministic action caching and replay artifacts
ground_truth: Repeated runs should bypass agent execution when the same verified input has already been solved.
scope: Implement SQLite-backed action caching keyed by frontend IR, base commit, and constitution hash.
Store replayable patches and verification summaries.
Expose a runtime API for cache hit and miss handling.
acceptance: Cache keys are deterministic.
Verified patches can be loaded without rerunning execution.
Cache persistence survives process restarts.

work_items.title: Build async pass manager and saga-based pipeline orchestration
ground_truth: The repo currently relies on a sequential materialization shell script and generated session packages.
The target architecture requires explicit passes and compensation for side effects.
scope: Add an async PassManager and Pipeline that lower requests and orchestrate execution.
Implement Saga registration and reverse-order compensation.
Support cache replay before agent execution.
acceptance: Pass order is explicit and testable.
Failures trigger compensation in reverse order.
Pipeline outputs include cache, verification, and event metadata.

work_items.title: Add contract-based verification with fail-to-pass and pass-to-pass stages
ground_truth: Verification must prove the targeted bug fails on the base state before the patch and passes after the patch.
Full-suite or selected regression checks must also remain green after the patch.
scope: Implement a verification runner for pytest selectors and metric thresholds.
Return structured verification evidence instead of raw command output only.
Integrate verification before cache population.
acceptance: Fail-to-pass checks detect already-green bug selectors.
Pass-to-pass checks block regressions.
Verification summaries can be serialized into cache and events.

work_items.title: Implement the andon watcher for authorized file enforcement
ground_truth: Execution must stop immediately when writes occur outside the authorized file list.
scope: Add an authorization watcher that validates touched paths against the execution plan whitelist.
Surface security violations as first-class runtime errors.
Integrate watcher checks into patch replay and agent execution paths.
acceptance: Allowed paths pass cleanly.
Disallowed paths raise a security violation.
Violations are included in pipeline event output.

work_items.title: Add content-addressed experiment workspaces and event store logging
ground_truth: Autoresearch-style iterations should not rely on shared mutable directories or hard resets.
Each experiment should carry a full event trail and IR snapshot.
scope: Implement a content-addressed worktree manager for git repositories.
Add a JSONL event store for execution, verification, and compensation events.
Emit worktree and event metadata from the pipeline.
acceptance: Worktree paths are derived from stable hashes.
Event records append as JSONL with event type and payload.
Cleanup paths are available for saga compensation.

work_items.title: Integrate runtime with scaffolding flow, generated tickets, and local tests
ground_truth: The existing run kit still materializes lowering-pass and factory artifacts from JSON config.
The new runtime should coexist with that flow and emit updated ticket slices.
scope: Add a CLI entrypoint for the runtime pipeline.
Update the generated queue inputs to use the new compiler slices.
Add unit tests covering the runtime modules and pipeline behavior.
acceptance: The repo materializes updated ticket artifacts from lowering_config.json.
The runtime can be invoked from a local CLI.
Local tests cover cache, watcher, saga, verification, and pipeline paths.

review.title: Compiler Runtime Verification Gates
review.intro: > These gates verify the runtime modules and generated artifacts for the compiler-spec refactor.
how_to_use: Map each work item to a risk tier in factory.yaml.
Run the required gates before marking an item complete.
Escalate only when a human approval condition is explicitly met.

gates.title: Type Safety
gates.pass_condition: All runtime and tool modules compile
gates.failure_action: Stop and return the errors to the implementing agent.
checks: No syntax or unresolved import errors remain.

gates.title: Happy Path Verification
gates.failure_action: Block merge until the main flow is working.
checks: Pipeline cache replay and execution paths are covered.
Watcher, saga, and verification rules remain intact.

gates.title: Scope Discipline
gates.pass_condition: Change stays inside the compiler-runtime wedge
gates.failure_action: Cut scope or split into a later item.
checks: No new ad hoc orchestration path bypasses the IR and verifier layers.
The implementation still matches the compiler-spec architecture.

merge_protocol: Check the implementation against CLAUDE.md invariants.
Reject work that widens scope without a research-backed reason.

factory.description: Compiler-runtime refactor slices with explicit caching, verification, security, and pipeline orchestration.
